facts.text: Canton Valley’s roots stretch back to the Massaco people of the Tunxis tribe, with archaeological sites dating to 4,000–2,000 B.C.
The village grew around the Albany Turnpike in the early 1800s, becoming a bustling stagecoach stop that hosted churches, schools, and cider mills.
Ephraim Mills named the town after Swiss cantons, a nod to his admiration for Swiss patriotism and territorial organization.
Every summer, Canton Valley’s Mills Pond Park hosts a concert series that spotlights local artists and musicians.
The Canton Land Conservation Trust protects over 2,000 acres, offering hiking, fishing, kayaking on the Farmington River, and biking along its trail.
The historic Collins Company, a 19th‑century industrial powerhouse, remains a landmark and a beacon for visitors and innovators alike.
